RESPONSE TO DEFTS PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND REQUEST FOR STAY OF PROCEDINGS FILED BY PLAINTIFF HAMPTON, GREGORY J.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAWYERS' MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND REQUEST FOR STAY OF PROCEEDINGS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY, IS GRANTED. COSTS AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $785. THE POLICY REQUIRES ARBITRATION OF COVERAGE ISSUES AND ISSUES OF DUTY TO DEFEND ARE SUBSUMED THEREUNDER. THIS IS MANIFESTED BY THE STRUCTURE OF ARTICLE 2 OF THE POLICY. THE DUTY TO DEFEND DEPENDS ON THE SCOPE OF COVERAGE. MANSDORF IS NOT ON POINT. THERE THE AGREEMENT EXPRESSLY CONTEMPLATED LEGAL ACTIONS ON SOME ISSUES FOLLOWING EARLIER ARBITRATION. ANY AMBIGUITIES IN THE SCOPE OF ARBITRATION ARE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF COVERAGE. TAYLOR V. CRANE, 24 CAL.3D 442, 450, WEEKS V. CROW, 113 CAL.APP.3D 350, 353. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay Of Proceedings;
Off Calendar Mar-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAWYERS' MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay Of Proceedings.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. Motion to compel arbitration and stay, is granted. Costs awarded in the amount of $785. The policy requires arbitration of coverage issues and issues of duty to defend are subsumed thereunder. This is manifested by the structure of Article 2 of the Policy. The duty to defend depends on the scope of coverage. Mansdorf is not on point. There the agreement expressly contemplated legal actions on some issues following earlier arbitration. Any ambiguities in the scope of arbitration are resolved in favor of coverage. Taylor v. Crane, 24 Cal.3d 442, 450, Weeks v. Crow, 113 Cal.App.3d 350, 353. (302/CK/VC) JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW, CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 9689
